title: Fusion methods for hybrid search
description: How reciprocal rank fusion and weighted score combination merge lexical and vector result lists.
body: Use when merging lexical and vector result sets. Reciprocal rank fusion combines rankings by summing 1/(k+rank), avoiding the need to calibrate incomparable score scales; weighted score fusion blends normalized scores but requires tuning the weight and normalization. Do not naively add raw BM25 and cosine scores — their scales differ and one dominates. Actions: prefer RRF for robustness or tune weighted fusion on a labeled set, and benchmark against each component. Validation signals include fused recall beating both single-mode baselines and a documented fusion configuration. Risk boundary: uncalibrated score fusion silently favors one retriever; if fusion underperforms a component, switch to RRF and re-evaluate before shipping.
